patches.find: (i) The R<sub>p</sub> ∝ h<sub>Moon</sub> linear scaling assumed throughout this section is the parallel-ray approximation the dome's own eclipse page uses; under finite-source umbra geometry the scaling becomes weakly non-linear, but the corrected ratio at h<sub>Moon</sub> ≈ 246,000 km still exceeds 90× the dome's published 2,534 km, so the kill is unaffected.
patches.replace: (i) R<sub>p</sub> ∝ h<sub>Moon</sub> is the wholesale-rescaling answer: exact only if h<sub>Sun</sub>, h<sub>Moon</sub>, and both body radii are all scaled together by the same factor (97.24× in this case). Under that reading, R<sub>p</sub> = 4,084 km requires h<sub>Moon</sub> = 246,401 km AND h<sub>Sun</sub> = 557,466 km. Under partial scaling — h<sub>Sun</sub> held at the published 5,733 km, R<sub>Moon</sub> tracking the angular-size match — the exact finite-source formula R<sub>p</sub> = R<sub>M</sub> + (R<sub>S</sub> + R<sub>M</sub>)·h<sub>M</sub>/(h<sub>S</sub> − h<sub>M</sub>) diverges as h<sub>Moon</sub> → h<sub>Sun</sub>; reaching R<sub>p</sub> = 4,084 km then requires h<sub>Moon</sub> = 5,660 km, putting the Moon within 73 km of the firmament Sun itself. The dome's published parameter set cannot produce a 4,084-km penumbra under either reading; the kill is unaffected under linear charity and strengthened under exact geometry.
patches.rationale: EXP-407: ISS-1643 (EXP-260.c1 H4). Replaces hand-wavy 'weakly non-linear' footnote with two-reading exact analysis (wholesale rescaling vs partial scaling). Conclusion upgrades from 'unaffected' to 'unaffected under linear charity, strengthened under exact geometry'. 51→155 words.

patches.find: Coimbra–San Pablo at Δr = 150 km — already outside an 85-km window.
patches.replace: Coimbra–San Pablo at Δr = 150 km — already outside an 85-km window. (Under the stricter R<sub>disc</sub>/180 projection convention treated in the P1 robustness paragraph above, this pair's Δr halves to ≈ 75 km, and Ebro–Coimbra to ≈ 82 km — both inside the 85-km band — which softens the constant-r reading to "max 2" rather than max 1; the 0/9 headline survives either convention.)
patches.rationale: EXP-409: ISS-1747 (SEC-4.2.7a EXP-276.c2 H4). Adds inline parenthetical cross-referencing P1 robustness paragraph, making R_disc/90 vs R_disc/180 convention dependency explicit at the point where the 150 km claim appears.

patches.find: reading the 10–13 µGal target through κ inversely — Δg × κ = ΔB — back-implies an unstated August 2026 ΔB premise of 16.8–21.8 nT at the named target stations (10 µGal × 1.677 nT/µGal = 16.77 nT; 13 µGal × 1.677 nT/µGal = 21.80 nT).
patches.replace: applying κ to the 10–13 µGal target yields a back-implied August 2026 ΔB premise of 16.8–21.8 nT at the named magnetometer stations along the August 2026 eclipse path. (Arithmetic: 10 µGal × 1.677 nT/µGal = 16.77 nT; 13 µGal × 1.677 nT/µGal = 21.80 nT.)
patches.rationale: SEC-6.14 c5 H3+H4+H5: (H3) Remove misleading 'inversely' — Δg×κ=ΔB is the same coupling rearranged, not an inverse. (H4) Tighten 'named target stations' to 'named magnetometer stations along the August 2026 eclipse path'. (H5) Break ~80-word nested-clause sentence into lead claim + arithmetic parenthetical.

patches.find: A defender who assumes the dome takes a typical-ensemble ΔB of 10–13 nT for August 2026 finds that 10/1.677 ≈ 6.0 µGal and 13/1.677 ≈ 7.8 µGal — both below the 10–13 µGal SG-pulse target.
patches.replace: Even at the ensemble upper edge of 15 nT, ΔB/κ = 15/1.677 ≈ 8.94 µGal — still ~1 µGal below the 10 µGal lower target. This is the closest a defender can get while remaining ensemble-bounded; 8.94 µGal vs 10 µGal is within the ±2 µGal per-station uncertainty bands the dome publishes, so the ensemble-bounded defense is live and the dome can credibly argue the shortfall disappears under station-level uncertainty.
patches.rationale: SEC-6.14 c5 H2: Replace understated defender example (10–13 nT sub-range) with honest ensemble upper bound (15 nT → 8.94 µGal, only 1 µGal short). This acknowledges the dome's strongest available retreat while showing it still falls short.

patches.find: The arithmetic is internally consistent only if the dome commits to a ΔB premise above the typical ensemble; that premise itself is unpublished in any dome material we have located. Either the dome publishes its quantitative August 2026 ΔB prediction in advance (in which case the κ derivation becomes auditable in advance — a symmetric strengthening of the vise) or it does not (in which case the 10–13 µGal target's derivation has an additional unspecified premise beyond κ). Both horns lose at least one WIN.
patches.replace: The dome's predictions.html publishes per-station August 2026 ΔB predictions under two baselines: BOU 2017 (−10.9 nT) and W004 empirical (−22.24 nT). Under BOU baseline, the per-station predictions (range −5.0 to −12.8 nT) through κ yield Δg of 3.0–7.6 µGal — well below the 10–13 µGal target. Only the W004 baseline yields Δg consistent with the target: W004 values at the 9 eclipse-path stations range from −10.3 to −26.2 nT, putting Δg at 6.1–15.6 µGal; the high-coverage central stations (Ebro, San Pablo, Coimbra, Chambon, Hartland) land in or above the 10–13 µGal range. The dome is implicitly committed to W004. W004's −22.24 nT is ~1.5× the upper edge of the Kim & Chang 2018 typical totality ensemble (5–15 nT) and is derived from a single eclipse event, not from the 207-measurement global ensemble. The vise tightens: the dome's arithmetic is internally consistent only if W004 is honored as the operational baseline — moving the question from "is the premise published" to "is the W004 premise defensible." Both horns lose at least one WIN.
patches.rationale: SEC-6.14 c5 H1 (major): Remove factually wrong 'unpublished' claim. The dome's predictions.html explicitly publishes per-station ΔB under BOU and W004 baselines. W004 (-22.24 nT) is the implicit operational baseline and sits ~1.5× above the Kim & Chang ensemble upper edge. Reframing strengthens the vise: 'published but anomalous' beats 'unpublished and unverifiable'.
